channel.title: Posts on teddywrites
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on teddywrites

item.author: Teddy
item.content:
2025 Was a weird year. I wanted it to be a much better one. It had everything I needed.

January,

I got promoted, I was happy. I was looking forward to being much happier.

February,

I fell in love. After a few long years, I did. I didn’t know I had it in me. I was happier, maybe that was the happiest I’ve been in awhile. It was perfect.

March,

Love continued, Work was fun. To be in love is something to be cherished. But little did I know back then how naive I was.

April,

Then it began, the twists and turns. The stars and moons, the planets and time and the downfall. The sorrow, the grief, the neglect. Every negative thing you can come up with, it was there.

May,

Silence, Once I loved it, and now I embraced it. I knew this was it. I was just holding on to a pipe dream. When you know, you know. It’s weird how even when we know, how blind love can make us. I was no different.

June, July, August, September,

I don’t want to go through months, It was the same shit all over again. And on a fateful day in September, It ended. No tears left to cry, no hoots left to give, It just ended. Like it never happened in the first place.

October,

5 stages of grief, I was zooming through it. Therapy, for the first time in my life. Worked. I was fine and healing.

November,

Flood, unexpected. But we’re used to it. The flood aftermath was weird. It was a mess, cleanup was easy but replying to messages was not. But, it fixed itself.

December,

Slowly getting there. Hopeful for the new year. Recovering, healing and looking for something more meaningful in life. Maybe a purpose.

item.title: One Last Time - Revisiting
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I’ve always wondered how fragile promises can be. How at one time we are so fixated on trying to make everything and then suddenly those promises mean nothing at all. One time someone could mean the world to us and then they mean nothing at all.

How fleeting is this feeling of love, where it can both heal you and break you to a point of no return. When Franz Kafka said that Love is the knife I turned inside me, I was skeptical but now I know how it feels to be Kafka. When I said Kafka is me, I never thought that I’d be like him after all. All I need to do is post a series of notes I’ve written to my girlfriend, if that’s even what I can call her.

Wake-up notes to an Unicorn, I named it. I wanted her to wake up and read one of my notes everyday. I loved writing those. Maybe she loved them as well, maybe. I can’t really speak for her.

item.title: One Last Time
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Hey, how are you?

She sent me a text.

I had been expecting it. It had been a while.

I’m just fine, I guess. Nothing good, nothing bad. Just fine, I replied.

Oh, I know those words. You’re already planning how to cope if I leave. Why do you always overthink things?

She was hauntingly accurate when it came to reading my feelings.

It’s better to be prepared, I thought. That’s what always happens, isn’t it? People leave. It wasn’t that I was perfect—I had made my fair share of mistakes—but I wasn’t someone who had done nothing wrong either. Maybe they just felt trapped. Maybe I was the problem. But it’s just a hypothetical.

And you think I’ll leave? she asked.

Everyone does. And it won’t change. The words came out before I could stop them. That’s what I’m afraid of. That it’ll happen over and over again until there’s nothing left of me but a hollow shell.

Oh. Do you really think that of me? That I’d leave just because everyone else did?

And then, the phone rang.

She had never called me before. I stared at her contact picture, hesitating. Will it hurt to believe just one more time?

I’m not mad at you. Just pick up the phone. Her text came through, and then it rang again.

This time, I answered.

Hi, I said.

It’s going to be okay. We’ll figure it out. Don’t be so hard on yourself. We’ve both been through a lot alone. But now, we can figure things out together.

Her voice was calm, steady.

You should take your own advice sometimes. You need it, she added.

I let myself sink into the strange comfort of her voice. I could have said anything, but at that moment, words didn’t matter.

Yeah. Just once more.

I would try.

item.title: Walls
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Do you know what’s not fair? It’s when you leave a bad relationship and you are the one who’s left suffering. Trying not to feel anymore, trying to walk on eggshells around emotions because they’ve become so fragile that you don’t feel like you can entrust someone with them.

And then, you’ll put up countless walls because of different reasons. These walls will have different traumas and in the middle of those walls you will rest on a pedestal waiting for someone to approach you to tell you that things will be okay again.

But, as much as that’s what You want, for someone to come to you and tell you that everything will be alright, You will not allow them to enter that easily. The walls you’ll put will remain the same no matter how pure the intentions of the person who approaches them.

So, how is it fair that you are the one who’s suffering because someone else decided to hurt you? And even when they’ve moved on towards others, You’ll just stay there trying to fix something that shouldn’t have been broken in the first place.

But, these walls will fall eventually for the right one. As they approach You feel, Actually you won’t feel the walls disappearing. You’ll eventually notice that you are not alone on the pedestal anymore. As long as you’re comfortable with the person who you share the pedestal with, the walls won’t matter. Because, If it’s the right person, they will always be there beside you. It won’t be sudden but the best part of it is that you won’t notice it.

item.title: Revisiting Burnout
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I Hate Thinking

I always have. It drags me down a strange, twisting rollercoaster of emotions. No safety bar, no map, just a plunge into the unknown. I always end up in the same dark corner of my mind. A place crowded with what ifs, should haves, and why nots.

And I hate it there.

It’s noisy, uncertain, and heavy.

Still, no matter how hard I try to avoid it, I always find my way back. Sometimes, I go there without even realizing it. Just a quiet moment, a breath too long, a silence too wide. And suddenly, I’m spiraling again.

Work helps. Staying busy helps. Tasks, to-do lists, distractions, They build a dam against the flood. I stack my hours with noise, with purpose, with urgency. But even that has its limits. Eventually, the cracks show.

I can’t keep patching up the holes with productivity. I know what burnout feels like. It creeps in quietly at first, disguised as fatigue. Then it digs deeper, draining everything—motivation, joy, even the will to keep pretending.

Right now, I feel like I’m balancing on a thin, fragile line. One foot in survival, one foot in surrender. I’m not okay, but I’m not gone. Not yet.

Some days, I imagine disappearing. Just slipping away. No grand exit. No explanation. Quietly. Like a whisper no one notices until the silence becomes too loud.

I think about what it would mean to let go. Not in a dramatic way, but in a slow unraveling. A soft release. A break from the noise inside my head. A chance to breathe without fear of drowning in thought.

Maybe that’s what I’m really hoping for. Not escape… just peace. A version of quiet that doesn’t hurt.

item.title: Dose of Reality
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Have you ever been hit by a sudden dose of reality?

One moment, you’re calm and content. Next, you’re questioning everything you’ve ever done. And then, just like that, the thought creeps in, what if you’re not enough?

It doesn’t just pass. It lingers, gnawing at the edges of your mind, a constant hum of doubt that won’t quiet down. You try to drown it out with work, with noise, with anything that keeps your brain too busy to spiral. Because as long as you’re occupied, you’re fine. But the moment your mind is free to wander, the dread crashes in, heavy and suffocating.

You tell yourself it’s just overthinking, just a phase, just exhaustion, but deep down, it feels like something more. Like a shadow that follows you, always just a step behind, waiting for a quiet moment to make itself known. And the worst part? No one else sees it. On the outside, you seem fine. Functional. Maybe even happy. But inside, it’s a constant war between who you are and who you think you should be.

So you keep going. You keep moving, hoping that if you run fast enough, the thoughts won’t catch up. That maybe, just maybe, one day they’ll disappear entirely.

But what if they don’t?

item.title: Eternal Dance of Life
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Hi.

“You’ve been awfully silent the past few days,” she said.

“Yeah, I have. Just had a lot on my mind.”

I think I’ve seen this before. You, me, and this eternal dance around the obvious. Last time, I barely survived the ending.

I always believed that one day, I’d be strong enough to go through it again. Turns out, I’m not.

Because at the end of this dance, you’ll have someone to fall back into. And I won’t.

That thought haunts me and keeps telling me that my suffering is justified, no matter how much it hurts.

You’re like the moon, Calm, alluring, untouchable. And I’ll fall for you all over again, knowing exactly how it ends. I’ll hate myself for it. I’ll lie in a pool of my own tears, I’ll pick up the pieces of my shattered heart, convince myself I’m fine. Picking apart the moments I should have noticed, Blaming myself, And, just like always, there won’t be anyone to tell me otherwise.

And then, one day, I’ll fall for someone else. And the cycle will begin again.

item.title: Talking with myself
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Hi, It’s me. Yeah I know. You only come to me when you’re struggling with your emotions and shit. Hey, be kind. I’m you. You can’t be that mad at yours.. Oh. yeah. Now you get it. Fine, What did I do this time? Nothing. And? Nothing. That’s the issue. You had everything perfectly aligned to your happiness and you did nothing. I thought that’s the point when things are lining up? No you fucking Idiot. Nobody can believe that shit. You need to do something in order for things to happen. Oh. You fucking donkey. Now what? Now Wha.. Are you fucking listening to me? Or Are you just that dumb. Hey, again, I’m you. Be kind. I have things going on and I don’t have time to be rational everytime. That’s why you’re here. Ok fine. Ugh, It irks me to say this but I think for now, Just wait. Don’t screw it up more than you have already done.

item.title: Unwritten Story
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
There’s an unwritten story about us. The reason it’s not written is that either one of us is afraid to pick up the pen and write it. We’ve both been so close to picking up that pen but we’d rather let the other one start the story. And until that moment, we’re going to have this infinite staredown at each other, a metaphorical staredown where nobody will win.

So, the one who picks up the pen first will dictate the terms of it. They will establish how this will flow but both of us are afraid of it aren’t we?

Because picking up the pen means taking responsibility for what happens next. It means putting feelings into words, making them real, and once they’re real, they can’t be ignored.

We dance around the edges of something that could be, waiting for the other to be brave enough to take the first step. Maybe it’s fear of rejection, fear of change, or simply the comfort of the unknown. Because once the story starts, we can’t pretend we don’t know how it began.

But here’s the thing—stories don’t write themselves. If we keep waiting, this story will remain unwritten, a lingering possibility instead of a reality. And maybe, just maybe, the only way to know how it ends is for one of us to finally take the pen and begin.

item.title: Note to Self
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
The magic of life is that you never truly realize when you’ve fallen in love—or when you’ve fallen out of it. Some days, you hold onto hope, believing that if you just try a little harder, things will fall into place. You convince yourself that happiness is within reach, just one decision away. And then, one day, you wake up and realize that no matter how much you hoped, no matter how much effort you put in, some things simply aren’t meant to last.

A wise man once said, “People say time changes things, but it’s not true. Doing things changes things. Not doing things leaves things exactly as they were.” Life doesn’t change just because you wait for it to. You witness countless moments unfolding around you. Some you notice, some you don’t. But only a few are within your control. And when you lose control of those, whether through action or inaction, you alone are responsible for the consequences, be they good or bad.

It’s easy to blame circumstances, timing, or even other people, but the truth is, life is nothing more than a reflection of the choices you make. Every decision, no matter how small, leads you down a path that shapes who you become. Sometimes, that path is clear and purposeful. Other times, it feels like you’re wandering without direction. But even in the moments of uncertainty, one thing remains true: you are in charge of your own life.

item.title: Chaos
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Is she chaotic?

No. She’s not. I don’t know how to describe her though. She feels so homely. Is that even a word? I feel at ease with her. I’ve felt this way before but to feel that comfort again out of nowhere in a random encounter feels so weird.

Should I?

I don’t really know. I’m torn between two options. What if she’s not that into me. I don’t want to stop feeling this. But then again to have it around you at all times seems like a fair trade-off. Why is it so hard to make decisions when it involves myself?

item.title: Love, Time, and Messages
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It finally happened. It was only funny when I was theorycrafting about it, but now that it really happened, I don’t know how to handle this.

Have you ever thought about or even dreamt of time travel? Or something similar? Well, not exactly traveling, but some kind of phenomenon related to time manipulation.

It happened to me—well, in a way. It happened to her as well. The issue is that only one of us knows. Well, technically two, because she counts as two. For fuck’s sake, time travel is complicated.

Let me start simply and keep it short.

I was messaging this girl I met a few days ago. She was funny and interesting—right up my alley, you know. Out of nowhere, I started receiving messages via two different chats in my chat app. At first, I didn’t think much of it because it seemed like a quirky glitch. But after a few days, it hit me—slowly, though.

In one chat, the most recent one, she was far more flirty and familiar. It felt like we’d known each other for years. I brushed it off as her usual funny self, but it was more than that. I got curious, and being the idiot I am, I asked her directly. Our conversation went somewhat like this:

Me : Hey, so I was gonna ask you something.

Her : Yes, what is it now?

Me : Um, this might sound weird, though.

Her : Okay, I guess. It’s not like we’re strangers or anything. And I know you, so nothing can weird me out about you.

Me : See, that’s the thing. That… ugh. Okay, who are you?

Her : What do you mean? Is this one of your pranks? I’m [redacted].

Me : Okay, but why are you being so flirty? We’ve known each other for, like, 10 or 15 days now.

Her : 10 or 15 days? What the actual fuck, man. Is this a joke?

Me : No, not really.

Her : Oh… oh. You’re serious. Oh no, that only means one thing.

Me : What?

Her : No, this can’t be happening.

Me : What can’t be happening?

Her : Okay, you’re a smart guy, right?

Me : Umm… I think so? Maybe?

Her : Listen to me very carefully. There’s been a little issue with our time manipulator. Don’t think too hard about it. It’s from the future and nothing you need to worry about. What I want you to do now is don’t tell the other me anything. Can you promise me that?

Me : Okay.

Her : That’s it? Just okay? No questions like “What’s a time manipulator?” or “When are you from?” Nothing?

Me : I mean, if it’s something related to time, I’ll eventually know about it, right? Less I know is better?

Her : Smart boy. That’s why I married you.

Me : Sorry, what?

Her : Oops. Spoilers. I’ll fix this so you won’t be able to message this chat anymore. It’ll remove itself from your device for… reasons. But hey, it was nice peeking into the past. Save yourself for me, okay?

Me : Wait, married? What?

Although I sent the message, it didn’t deliver, and I couldn’t find the chat afterward. It was the most surreal few minutes of my life up to that point. I almost chalked it up to a prank, but a chat suddenly disappearing like that kind of made it convincing.

So, married. Who would’ve thought?

Come to think of it, I guess I will in the future.

But now, I can’t stop thinking about what she said. Time manipulator? A future me she’s already married to? I’m torn between excitement and dread. If this is real, what does it mean for the choices I make from now on?

I’ve always been curious about the future, but now I’m a little scared to meet it. Still, if she’s right, maybe… just maybe, the future’s looking pretty good after all.

item.title: 2 Numbers
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It finally happened. It was only funny when I was theorycrafting it but after it really happened now I don’t know how to handle this.

Have you ever thought about or even dreamt about time travel? Or something of sorts. Well, not exactly travel but some kind of phenomenon related to time travel.

It happened to me, well in some sort of way, It happened to her as well, the issue is only one of us knows. Well, technically, two because she counts as two. For fucks sake, Time travel is complicated.

So let me start this simply and keep it short.

I was messaging this girl I met a few days ago. She was funny and interesting. Right up in my alley you know and out of nowhere I started receiving messages via two different chats in my chat app. At first I didn’t really mind cause It was funny. But after a few days it hit me, slowly though.

In one chat, the most recent one, she was far more flirty and homely. It was likely we’ve known each other for some years. I brushed it off as her usual funny self but it was more than it. So, I became curious and as the idiot I am I asked her directly. Our conversation went somewhat like this.

Me - Hey, So. I was gonna ask you something.

Her - Yes, what is it now?

Me - Um, This might sound weird though.

Her - Okay, I guess. It’s not like we’re strangers or anything. And I know you so nothing can weird me out about you.

Me - See, that’s the thing. That.. ugh. Okay, Who are you?

Her - What do you mean? Is this one of your pranks? I’m redacted. She said her name.

Me - Okay, Why are you being flirty? We’ve known each other for like 10 or 15 days now.

Her - 10 or 15 days, what the actual fuck man. Is this a joke?

Me - No, No. not really.

Her - Oh, oh… you’re serious. Oh no, that only means one thing.

Me - What?

Her - No, This can’t be happening.

Me - What can’t be happening?

Her - Okay, you’re a smart guy. Right?

Me - umm.. I think so. Maybe.

Her - Listen to me very carefully. There has been a little issue with our time manipulator. Don’t think about it. It’s somewhere in the future and nothing to be concerned with. What I want you to do now is don’t tell the other Me anything. Can you promise me that?

Me - okay.

Her - That’s it? Just okay? No questions like What is a Time manipulator, when are you? Anything?

Me - I mean, If it’s something related to time, It has to be the future so I will eventually know about it. Less I know is better? Right?

Her - smart boy, that’s why I married you.

Me - Sorry what?

Her - Oops! Spoilers. I’ll fix this up so You won’t be able to message this chat anymore. It’ll remove itself from your device because of the reasons. But hey, It was nice peeking into the past. Save yourself for me okay?

Me - Wait, Married? What?

Although I sent the message, It didn’t deliver and I couldn’t find the chat afterwards. It was the most surreal few minutes I ever had in my life up to that point. I was gonna think that as a prank but a chat suddenly disappearing kinda made it convincing.

So, Married. Who would’ve thought.

Come to think of it, I will in the future.

item.title: Oldself
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I often stumble upon people telling me that they miss their old selves. Funny they are. I’ve always wanted to ask them whether they even remembered who they used to be. I mean, I can’t remember who the fuck I was yesterday let alone my oldself. How do people keep saying that they miss someone when they clearly have no idea who they were before?

Maybe I’m too grumpy after not writing anything for a long time but to me, missing my old self is nothing but a weird excuse for the person who they’ve become. Why would you want to go back if you’re a better person than before? The only reason you miss or wish to go back to who you once were is because either you don’t like who you’ve become or you feel like you’ve failed your own expectations. Either way, that’s not something you can fix by praying or wishing to be your oldself.

item.title: Liquid Courage
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
ring ring ring

“Hello?” a voice on the other end replied.

“Hey, man, it’s me, Max,” I enthusiastically said.

“Holy fu… Where the hell have you been? It’s been a hot minute, hasn’t it? When did we last talk?” he asked.

“I can’t remember, really. Probably a year ago, I guess?” I replied.

“It should be. So why did you suddenly manifest? What’s going on with your life, man?” he was curious.

“Ah, nothing much. Remember back when I used to call you in the middle of the night because I remembered some random thing? And you were like, ‘What the heck, man? It’s like 3 in the morning. Just let me sleep.’” I laughed as I told him that.

“Of course, how can I forget? You were the baby I never had. Waking me up suddenly just to say some random bullshit. Hahaha, good times, man, good times. But you never gave me an answer for my earlier question,” he reminded me.

“Ah, yes. I just suddenly remembered how you were there for me for most of my life. When I was going through so many hardships. I just wanted to thank you. I know it’s untimely, but sooner is better than never, isn’t it?” I replied.

“Yes, that’s true. I guess,” he was a bit dumbfounded from my sudden compliment bomb.

“Okay, dude, I gotta go. I just randomly remembered and decided to let you know. I’ll ring you back when I’m free.” I hung up the phone after sharing pleasantries with him.

“That’s one down,” I thought to myself.

“Now on to the next one.”

ring ring

“Max?” a gentle voice was at the other end of the phone.

“Hey, Zara. I know it’s weird to call you out of nowhere,” I said.

“I mean, you’ve never called me before. This is the first time. So, what’s up?”

“Zara, I wanted to tell you something. Something I desperately wanted to get out of my chest.”

“Umm, okay, go on.” She was reluctant to express her doubts about my intentions.

“I had… No. I have a huge crush on you. It’s me, well maybe with added liquid courage talking to you at the moment. I drank for the first time, and now I’m speedrunning my life. I love you! That’s pretty much it,” I blurted out.

“Max, I know you’re more than tipsy at the moment, and this is not something you drop on someone out of nowhere. Just sober up, and we’ll talk about this later. Okay?” she told me.

“Well, you didn’t decline me, that’s a start. Woo-hoo, go me! Hey, Zar…”

beep beep beep

“Well, look at that, I left her speechless.”

“That’s about it. Now, it’s me and Liquid Courage,” I told myself while looking at the vodka bottle in my hand.

I stood up and stretched my body for a while.

“I always wanted to find an answer to the question: When tested against gravity, what’s heavier? A sack of meat filled with emotional baggage and guilt or a bottle of vodka? I guess I’ll find out in a while.”

item.title: Alternate Adolf
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
This, This doesn’t have enough heads you know. But. you’ve managed to capture the essence of architecture really well. Don’t you think that’s your calling? One of the instructors said to the teen who was standing in front of him expectantly.

Sir, I was…

I’m sorry but We are looking for someone with other talents than yours. I strongly believe your calling is Architecture.

item.title: Tired
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I’m a little bit tired.

I was procrastinating for a while, well that’s very mildly put to be honest. I haven’t written anything lately.

I was busy, yes. But I think it’s mostly due to not having anything to write about.

These feelings are quite funny. They catch up to us when we weren’t expecting. All they need is a slight slip of reality to come rushing in.

So, I guess I’m not invulnerable to it. I thought I was.

I wanted to write about her. Not you, her.

The one who was there for me when no one knew what I was going through. I felt at ease when I shared everything with her, and little did I know I was in it for the long run, or so I thought.

Turns out, Things don’t work the way you want. There will always be a catch. And I was caught in it. And now, I’m one less soulmate in my life.

P.S. - I wish everyday we would’ve met under different circumstances, and I was a slightly better person, but We can’t change the past and Future is meh.

item.title: Dreams
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Do you remember the first time you let someone pay you to suppress your dreams?

Do you remember the second time? The third? And the many times after those?

Now, When are you gonna stop?

Do you think It’s worthwhile to get paid to stop dreaming?

Forget about dreams, Are you happy doing that?

item.title: The Doppelganger
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Louis was practising a new sheet music given by his mentor. He was always so fond of the piano and the music. In his journey of 5 years, he kept setting milestones to overcome previous ones he set. His mentor knew this and nurtured and trained him in a way where he didn’t feel overwhelmed.

Louis was happy with what he was given, He always wanted to play a piece by Liszt. As he sat down to play he could hear faint piano music coming from next door. He stopped for a moment and listened. It was as he guessed, someone playing a piano. He could hear the faint tune of river flows in you.

As he was listening to the music, it stopped as the one who was playing expected him to continue where he left off. So, he did. Louis was enjoying the tune. It was something he kept playing over and over again so he knew it by memory. While he was concentrating on playing he thought of what happened earlier and stopped his playing. In a mere moment, it was picked off by the person who was playing in the other room.

Louis was dueting off with a complete stranger and it made him ecstatic.

After the song was done, Louis went to bed thinking of what happened. Although he forgot about the sheet music his mentor gave him, He has a newfound love for the stranger on the other wall. As he drifts off to sleep, he could hear the music serenade through his ears.

Louis woke up to the warmth of the sun. He was laid off from his job a few days ago and his only coping mechanism was playing piano. It kept his mind off of everything else as he job hunted on the internet. After another disappointing search, Louis sat down to play, this time he could hear Clair de Lune, another one of his favourites. As before, it quickly turned out to be a back and forth play off between the stranger and Louis. He enjoyed every second of this new experience.

After the play, Louis stepped out for a bit of fresh air, as he passed the next door where his playing partner lives, he wanted to knock on the door. But something stopped him from doing it, maybe it was the anonymity he has, not knowing who it was that brought another interesting addition to Louis’s playtime. He walked outside to breathe in some fresh air as he enjoyed the past two days of his playtime.

When he was coming back to his room, he saw the door ajar of his neighbour.

“Maybe a peek won’t hurt?” Louis thought to himself.

He knocked on the door and announced his presence as he walked inside. But, there was no sign of someone living inside the room. As he opened the door to the room adjacent to his room on the other side. Louis was shocked with what he saw.

It was like a Mirrored room, it was exactly like Louis’s room but without him in it. He rushed back outside in disbelief. He went to his room and sat on the piano stool thinking about what he saw. As he was trying to figure it out, he heard a faint piano music from the other side, this time, it was a new tune he had never heard before. Curious, he took the sheet music he was given by his mentor a few days ago, “Der Doppelgänger” it read on top of the sheet. On top of the piano music, he could hear the singing voice of someone.

Louis didn’t know what he was experiencing. He went pale with everything that happened in the last few moments. If only he knew what was behind this truly magnificent piece that was playing inside his head.

item.title: Creator of Death
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
As I finished my final entry on the report of the Olympian experiment, I felt like a heavy burden lifting from my shoulders. But that only lasted for a mere moment when I realised what I had done.

Are you okay Doctor? I heard the faint voice of my Assistant Veronica. It was only then I realised the worries had manifested to lengths where it can be seen from outside.

I’m okay Veronica, Tell me something. I paused.

Yes, What is it? Doctor. Veronica replied in an instant.

Do you think parents of Mass murderers were worried or regretted that they birthed and raised their kids to be such monsters? I asked.

I don’t know how to answer that, Doctor. But, I’m sure this won’t turn out to be anything like that. The core of the experiment is to prevent them from being sentient. I believe we got that right. Veronica assured me.

That’s true but after all things considered, It’s only letters and numbers that can be manipulated. I feel like I’m getting sick. This is not how I imagined the conclusion would be. I told as I was battling nauseating effects on my brain.

It will be okay Doctor. Look at me, I was your first. And I turned out to be okay. I don’t feel like killing anyone else. Veronica was worried about me. That’s what I wanted to think but Olympians weren’t given any emotional capacity to feel or express feelings. I was tasked to use every bit of their memory as efficiently as possible and they affirmed that emotions are not necessary.

We are going to turn a new page in Humanity, Doctor. When this Olympian project is successful, we are going to lure those rich suckers into buying a custom made kid. Yes, that’s how we are going to market this. DIY Kids for Rich folks. It’s almost like a trophy kid. We are gonna be rich. That’s what those who were funding the Research and Experiment said.

I felt sick. The Olympian project was my baby, the culmination of my efforts and It’s gonna be used to fund these bureaucrats and their schemes. I didn’t have a way out, I already used the entirety of the money they invested on this. If it weren’t for them Olympians would’ve been buried inside.

item.title: Metaphorical Web
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I’m stuck.

I’m stuck in a metaphorical web I created myself.

I’m stuck in need of constant validation to keep my motivation up and running.

I’m stuck in this self-made web of self constraints.

Did you know that spiders make these webs out of their Ass? I was probably thinking out of my ass when I created this metaphorical web as well.

It doesn’t change the fact that I’m still stuck.

It’s not that bad though, The web looks nice when an occasional compliment hangs off of it like a dew drop.

I find it comforting.

I feel like I’m suffering from Stockholm syndrome. I’m in love with this self-made web of constraints. This is my comfort zone, I don’t want to leave it.

I’m fine here.

I’m invincible here.

item.title: Death
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I have died, It’s been a minute.

I can hear Doctors and Nurses trying their best to breathe life into me. I feel an electric shock of Defibrillator coursing through my body. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

I have died, It’s been an hour.

I can hear my family trying to get into the theatre. They are at a loss and can’t believe what they heard. I see my wife and kids crying. They were good to me when I lived. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

I have died, It’s been a day.

I see my body is undergoing post mortem tests, They will send me to a funeral parlour where they remove my guts and viscera while making me presentable to those who visit my funeral. A thankless job. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

I have died, It’s been a week.

I have been back here at my home for awhile, casket isn’t the best place to lay down and sleep. They don’t know it. I saw many of my relatives come and go. I lost touch with most of them. Some of them still held grudges yet they came in anyway to save face. I saw my friends, they came in early and helped with everything, I was lucky to have them. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

I have died, It’s been a month.

I rest in a cemetery, the final destination I will ever have as a human being. My wife and kids visit me when they can. She cries sometimes, and my kids are too proud to do so. I see occasional visits from my best friend. He should move on, we’ve been through enough. I wanted to have one last drink. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

I have died, It’s been forever.

I wonder what happened with life. People rarely visit me. My wife does though. She has grown older than before. We were supposed to grow old together. I hope she is okay. I haven’t seen my kids though. I hope they are doing fine. I wanted to hug my wife one last time. But, I have accepted my death and moved on.

item.title: Loss
item.author: Teddy
item.content: “Logan! Logan!! Stay with me, don’t drift away.” Logan heard a faint voice in his ears, he could barely see a shadow of a woman through his bloodshot eyes. As he drifts to sleep, Logan knew this was it for him. He didn’t have time to process anything. As his final words, he chose to crack a joke at the woman he loved, “Drifting deez…” he couldn’t finish it as he succumbed to his ill fate.

item.title: Rain and Girl - Again
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I was caught off guard with a feeling of sudden coldness on my palm. It wasn’t in the forecast, was it? I didn’t even bring my umbrella. I thought to myself.

Although It was unexpected, I’ve always been a pluviophile. Ah, big word man.

The sky was grey, It was that darker shade of grey with earthy smell going around. If I remember correctly It’s been awhile since we had rain. I wasn’t in a hurry to get up from the bench I was sitting on.

Take it in, I said to myself.

When I’m all soaked in rain, I was no longer myself, I was one with rain.

I looked around to see what’s going on. I was expecting people looking at me and thinking what kind of a weirdo this is but that was not the case. No one was around. Well, except for this girl.

I stopped snooping around to stare at her.

Raindrops mixed with her eyeliner are racing down her rosy cheeks. I was betting on one drop to win the race as it drops down to her chest soon after. When I looked back up, she was looking at me.

Hi, she said.

Oh, hello. It’s raining isn’t it? I asked nervously.

She burst into laughter. I was caught off guard twice in the same day. This time, It’s her smile. I waited till she got over my nervous question earlier.

I’m sorry, It’s okay. And yes, It’s raining. She answered.

Yeah, that was a dumb question. Love rain? I asked.

I don’t love rain, I love rain today. It doesn’t make sense, I know. But I wanted it to rain today. She told me.

I noticed her headband slowly slipping off of her hair.

Well, I believe there’s an explanation for that. Not that I ask you to share. But, I hope rain helps. Otherwise if you get sick, this will all be for nothing. And you don’t want that. I told her.

Ah, I wouldn’t mind getting sick actually. I could use a break. She sighed. Well, in that case, You’ve come to the right place. I told her as I took my eyes off of her. She was there to be alone and who am I to disturb her.

Hey, thank you for not intruding. I appreciate it. She told me as she smiled back at me.

You’re welcome. I closed my eyes and drifted off to my own world. I love rain, I love it so much.

item.title: The Story of Harry
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
No, No. You don’t understand. She was trying to make amends. She knew what she did was wrong and all she wanted was a chance to prove that she deserved a new life as much as the next criminal who gets one. And this city opened up like a bottomless chasm and swallowed her whole. And you still think this is fair? James was screaming in the rain. A gun in his hand and droplets of water getting mixed downstream of tears on his face.

I do understand that James. What happened to Lily was not your fault. It happens. The world is cruel sometimes. But this is not the time for you to give in to your anger or resentment. It was not your fault. You have to calm down.

You weren’t there Thomas. She was happy. And the next moment she was gone. You still want me to stop this? James changed his aim. Now with the gun aimed at his temple James knew what’s next for him.

I’m gonna meet her on the other side, Thomas. This is it. Everything about us will end here. James cried at Thomas.

What about your child James? Thomas asked calmly.

Harry? Well, fuck him. I don’t wanna see his face. His eyes remind me of her. Fuck It. James told as he pulled the trigger. Thomas couldn’t do anything else but watch another one of his best friends die in front of his eyes. First It was Lily and now It’s James. Thomas knew that this was gonna be the result. James was never in his right mind and Thomas regretted that he couldn’t do anything to prevent it.

All he could do now is to hold on to the memory he has of their friends. His eyes went back to the child he was holding. He wasn’t even crying.

It’s okay, Harry. Let me tell you a story about a Magical world and Wizards.

You’re gonna love it.

item.title: The Spy
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Did you call me Mr. Evans? Timmy asked the bossy figure in front of him.

Ah, yes. Timmy, welcome my child. I hope I didn’t steal you from something important. Because This is as important as it gets. Mr. Evans asked Timmy.

No Sir. I was on my way here so It’s okay. Timmy, frightened, replied.

Now, Timmy. Let me ask you something. I’m gonna be honest with you for a second here. You know who I am, I’m a ruthless control freak. And I hate to see things out of order. Tell me Timmy, how do we treat someone who’s out of control? Mr. Evans asked in a menacing voice.

Timmy was holding on to the last bit of his nerves. He knew where this was going and there was nothing he could do that prevented it. His only option is to play along and hope for a miracle.

I’m waiting for Timmy, I don’t like that. Mr. Evans added.

Mmm… Get them back in or..

Yes, Get them back in order. Well done Timmy. Mr. Evans interrupted Timmy’s answer.

Now, A little bird told me that there’s someone inside my ranks who’s snitching. If I’m being honest, I don’t like snitches. I would’ve killed him on sight but unfortunately, I don’t know who he is. And I’m not gonna suspect anyone unless I get it confirmed.

Timmy, You’re new here. Nobody really knows who you are. Mr. Evans kept his rant going.

I want you to find the snitch Timmy. And tell me who he is. I’ll take care of the rest. To start, I want you to get to the Shamerand Dockyard, I lost some merchandise and some men there. You can sniff around to see whether there’s any clue to be found.

item.title: Dwyane on the Dancefloor
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Dwyane is still holding on to his laugh on the dancefloor. Although he’s feeling a bit woozy, He’s trying to process the event that just happened.

“Mr. Reinhardt sends his regards” Dwyane heard a faint voice in his left ear.

Dwyane could feel his shirt getting dampened by the sweat and something else. There was no pain, just a little bit of pressure.

“Why?” Dwyane asked.

He didn’t get any reply. As he was falling to the ground, Dwyane saw the shocking faces of those who were dancing around him. His decisions took him to the place he is at today.

In the corner of his eye, Dwyane saw a hooded figure walking away with a knife-like object drenched with his blood.

“Ah, Good for him. He’s gonna get rewarded.” Dwyane chuckled as he closed his eyes. “Maybe it’s not that bad, I can finally stop this game of cat and mouse.” he thought as he succumbed to eternal slumber.

item.title: Into the sunset - A Short story
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It isn’t easy, you know. Eventually, you’ll start to see me as I see myself right now, and the feelings you have for me will fade away like they never existed in the first place.

You see, love as we define it is relative to the opinion we have on someone. As long as you consider me as a proper option to share your time with and tolerate the existence of me beside you till death do us apart, you will continue to love me. If and when you lose that opinion, you’ll start to see the flaws you actively ignored till now.

The habits, traits, look, and all the other things you ignored because your opinion will haunt you over and over again till there’s nothing left of my image you had before.

Knowing this, will you be able to love me? If at some point in our lives, when you start to me as I see myself, would you start to hate me? Or would you be indifferent?

I spoke my train of thought without giving any consideration.

You know, you’re funny. She said.

Why? I had to ask.

I don’t want to see you in someone else’s eyes, not even yours. I see you as it is and I love you for who you are. The problems of the future are yet to happen. Can we just be true to ourselves and just enjoy our time together. We’ll face life as it comes. Don’t worry, I’ll be here to hold your hand if things get rough.

She stopped.

You’re weird. I told her as we both walked into sunset, talking about everything but love.

Photo by Sebastian Voortman on Pexels.com

Hi. I texted her.

Hey, how are you? It’s been a while, isn’t it? She replied.

I’m okay, I guess. Although I typed, I was hesitant to press that send key. Was I okay? Is it something that I can say to someone? I was never okay. But then again, anybody ever is? All of us have our battles we’re barely getting through. Some are winning it, some are losing it.

I pressed the send button.

I wanted to tell her about my demons and everything I’m going through, but she’s not someone I want to shift my burdens into. If I’m being honest, no one is.

We carry this immense burden hoping to put it down someday and be truly happy about life, but until then we’re destined to carry it. There are no shortcuts to happiness. I’ve experienced it, and it comes at a great cost.

You don’t get to be happy unless you’re ready for what follows. It may come sooner or later, but its arrival is inevitable. The sorrow and sadness that paired together with the happiness we acquired will be heavy for you to go through.

You can only hope that you won’t have to go through it in your youth. You simply won’t recover from it. Call it depression or whatever other fancy names you can come up with, but in the end, it’s just an emotional burden of your own.

The conversation went on as usual with her. Both of us not knowing about our demons, battling through it, yet trying to be a positive presence in each other’s lives because that’s what good people do or that’s what we think they do.

And I’m grateful for her. For not asking about them. Some might say that it’s wrong not to but to me, not sharing that makes it easier to keep us as to how it is now.

Do you want to meet up later? She asked.

Sure, why not. Same place? I need some sunset in my life right now. I replied.

Photo by Bruno Scramgnon on Pexels.com

Does love ever fade? I muttered.

Huh!? She got confused with the sudden question.

I mean, If we truly love someone, does it ever fade? The feelings you have of them. Even if it didn’t work out in the end, feelings would remain. And that residual feelings will hurt you over and over till you get tired of them. When I look at her, that’s pretty much the only thing I can think of. If we ever become strangers again, would I be able to go through with it?

Hey, Hello? Earth to Moon, can you hear me? Hi? She shook me out of my trance.

Are you okay? She asked.

Yeah, yeah I am. I told her.

She and I both know it’s a lie.

She has seen my scars. The ones above my veins, the ones she Always misread as an attempt at life. I wish it was that simple. I never wanted to attempt my life. All I wanted was to see whether I would be able to feel something again. I’ve become numb to feelings. Paint me a hypocrite, but it’s true.

The only reason I’m thinking about leaving her or her leaving me is to see whether I’d left with those feelings people talk about. The pain of losing someone you loved. Knowing they’d be happy with someone else, I want to feel it.

It’s getting late. I know you’re not okay. She spoke to me with a concerned voice.

I know you’ve been through a lot, and you’re looking for something that’ll keep you engaged with your mind and emotions. It will come to you soon. So, until that, I’m here with you. Whenever you need, just talk to me. You know where to find me. I’m always here. She kept going on.

I know. Thank you for that. I told her.

As the sun takes its final dive into the ocean, I was looking at two things I cherish the most.

A beautiful sunset and a beautiful soul.

Maybe it isn’t that bad. Maybe I’ll be happy One day.

Photo by Samuel Theo Manat Silitonga on Pexels.com

I woke up to a text chime on my phone.

Wake up, sleepyhead. It said.

I didn’t sleep the last few days. It’s not because I couldn’t, but I forced myself to. I’m not a unique being who sees Night as a time to stay unconscious. I enjoy the silence of dawn and the cold atmosphere it has. It’s not like a sunset but to me, I find it easier to feel at dawn. Even though I feel nothing at all, I can try without being distracted by sounds and other things that haunt me.

Hey, are you awake? I know you are. Reply me. The phone chimes again.

Yeah, I am. What’s up? Anything wrong? I asked out of habit.

Why do I assume that everyone is in some distress when they reach me? Maybe it’s because I think whether people genuinely need to message me. I’ve never had someone genuinely asked me how am I doing. Whenever I get a message, it’s always followed up with a request for help or something that doesn’t concern me.

That’s before I found her. She’s always going on about her or just randomly asks about me. It’s refreshing because she never asks or requests as other people do.

What? Do you think I’m like those people who think that something’s wrong with them all the time? You’re weird. She replied.

Yeah, yeah, I know. I’m sorry. I replied out of habit.

What are YOU sorry for? She replied.

She’s right. I shouldn’t be sorry for someone else, but when I feel something about her, it’s much more different from others. It feels so close to my heart, almost as if I’m feeling it about myself. If I were to say that love synchronizes the hearts of people, would you call me a liar? Probably. But that’s how I think it is. Sometimes I just can’t explain it.

A love that is not love.

A relationship that isn’t a relationship.

We both exist in it but at the same time, we don’t. That’s what intrigued me about her in the first place.

Photo by Thu00e1i Huu1ef3nh on Pexels.com

Hey, can I ask you something? She questioned.

Ask away. I replied.

When will this end? She asked.

When will what end? I was confused.

This. You, Me, and everything in between. She replied.

What!? What are you talking about? I kept asking

Never mind, it’s getting late. I told you not to sleep in the afternoon because I always have to wake you up for the evening. She went on.

Will you please get up and come to see me? She asked.

Although I didn’t understand the latter part of the conversation, I said yes. I wanted to see the sunset. I’ve been visiting outside and observing it more frequently than before.

Sunsets are mysterious sometimes. That’s one of the reasons I love it this much.

Sitting on the bench, looking at how the sun trying to keep itself above the horizon, made me think It’s almost as if it doesn’t want to go. But I wanted it to go and cover the world with darkness.

Sometimes I don’t understand how she is okay with my trances or how I zone out in the middle of conversations. Maybe that’s why I find her alluring. How she can tolerate my weird habits. It’s quite weird, isn’t it? How love can make us do things that we never thought we would.

Work for the extra mile, chase a dream that you never thought you had, do things that you never thought you wanted. You keep discovering these things in your life when you are with someone, and sometimes it’s rewarding.

As I looked up from my trance, the sun has given up its battle with the horizon. It was drowning in the sea, and it’s probably the most beautiful sunset I have seen so far. I felt something special about it. Just as I feel about this day.

Until tomorrow then? She said, snapping me out of my concentration towards the sun.

No. I guess it’s about time, isn’t it? I told her with a faint voice.

Oh, have you finally decided what to do with me? She asked with her angelic voice which I grew attached to.

Yes, It’s about time I let you go, isn’t it? It’s not working well for me. All these years clinging on to something that doesn’t even exist anymore. I was at the end of my sanity while I muttered those words.

But what about the sunsets we shared? Are you gonna stop it too? She asked.

No, I don’t think I will. I still love the sunset and I will keep watching them, feeling them, but here on out it won’t be with you.

I just wanted to say thank you. For all the memories in the past few days. Although it’s just me, it almost felt as if it’s someone else.

As I reach the medications I skipped a couple of days ago, I wanted to make sure that I’ll be able to survive this on my own.

Do you think I will be able to do that? I asked her.

I think so. You don’t have to doubt it. There are no doubts in your mind. You have to believe me because I am you. She assured.

Okay then, I guess this is goodbye. It’s kinda weird saying goodbye to yourself.

As I restarted my medications, I can feel her voice fading away and cease out of existence. I was on my own after a while.

I walked out to the beach, where I have always found my happy place. The sunset, the beach, and everything in between.

~ Fin ~

item.title: A little confession
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I’m writing this in case if things go south.

I was not a firm believer of spontaneous vibing where you meet a random person out of nowhere and you feel like you’ve known them for sometime. To me that’s what you are, someone who appeared out of nowhere with absolutely no fucking reason and it made sense.

I instantly forgot what happened last time when i forced this vibe thing to happen and I despised it although it wasn’t much of a nightmare. Like it or not it was not the kind of relationship that I wanted to be in and it definitely was not the relationship i have imagined.

Tbat being said, I have no idea who you really are or what you truly bring to the table. All i know is being where i am right now, i shouldn’t be leading you on to a disappointment. At this age, Love or a relationship is not something you can experiment with. So I’ve decided to do the excruciatingly painful thing of looking at your life through your moments. It’s what you call self torture and I’m so used to it.

This seems to be a pointless blabbering by another hopeless romantic, it needed to be put out there, because i don’t want anyone to be concerned about it. I’m perfectly calm and accepting of current decisions and the outcomes of it. You should be too.

Peace.

item.title: The Grind
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Dying is easy.

Contrary to what people say it’s not a hard decision to take with all the shit that’s going around. You can simply say that you had enough or you’re worthless and you’re left with no choice but end it all.

Do you know what’s hard though? Living. No matter whatever the shit you have try living for a change. It’s hard and you always want to give up but it’s worth everything. When you achieve things in life you’ll realise that you never would’ve felt it if you decide to end your life. That’s what life is a well rewarding grind that you got to endure. Not everyone will go through it but to those who do you’re awesome and keep doing whatever you’re doing to make it happen.

item.title: The girl on the train
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
No, it’s not a story about a childhood friend. I was a young boy when i first saw her. I was on the way to school on a train and with the puberty and the hormones world was a different place than before. and then there she was, the woman i felt attracted to. I don’t want to label it as love but there was something about her that’s compelling and it drew me to her. The exhausting journey on the train turned out to be an hour in heaven to me. My attendance went up at school and i just loved every moment i spent looking at her.

I didn’t know who she was, where she is from and where she’s going at the time. Just the fact of her existence in that hour i spent on train was everything to me. It was the most innocent a love can be. I was wearing my blue shorts and white shirt to the school and i see her wearing a saree that made her look stunning. It was pure bliss to me. For years she never even notice that i was there. She couldn’t see how starstruck i was everyday looking at her. She was in living in the real world but to me, in that instance, she was my entire world.

They say that when you grow up the love grows up with you as well. I’ve experienced it first hand with her. It was quite simple actually. A boy, a girl and a heavy load of unrequited feelings because of how afraid i was to lose this feeling. It bothered me since I’ve seen her. What if i confessed and it goes south, will i be able look at her again? What if she’s married or have feelings for someone else? What if she’s not interested? I was afraid of going back to what it was before her. The love that was unspoken kept me from boring to death on the journey and i wanted it to last, so i stayed in silence. She was one person in the world but for that moment, that moment only she was my whole world.

I was no longer a student. While i grew up everything around me changed as well. The feelings, emotions, people and everything that made me who i am. But the only thing that didn’t change was the unrequited love i had for someone i barely knew. Someone that probably saw me growing up with time, someone who probably saw my eyes watching her but definitely someone who never knew that i had feelings for her. To this day nothing has changed in life on that journey. I’ll see her often on my way to work. I’ll imagine countless ways to talk to her, I’ll dream of wild scenarios that may happen and then I’ll get off at my stop like nothing happened. There’s a saying that first love never leaves your heart and i never thought it’d be unrequited.

As long as both she and I exist in the train journey this love will stay and maybe, just maybe if i find courage to defy my bubble of dreams I may talk with her but I’m addicted to the love that’s unspoken so I’ll most likely stay in silence and cherish the memories of our journey together as separate beings.

The love that’s unspoken is the love that’s most cherished because of all the possibilities that can happen.

item.title: I feel nothing
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I can write thousands of stories filled with cheap romance and yet feel nothing of it. The words i write seems fake to me but i find people making sense of it. In reality it’s just a desperate attempt to create a feeling that I have lost throughout the time. It used to be so genuine and calming but now it feels forced. Sometimes i just want to believe that it is possible to lose feelings and be apathetic, i really do. But why does it feel like my heart breaks every time when something goes bad.

It makes no fucking sense to both feel and not feel the same feeling in same context every time. Why the fuck would i feel sad and happy at the same time about myself reverting back to being who i was few years back. It shouldn’t be like that, I don’t want it to be like that. Although i like the emotional detachment in it, it hurts my mind alot.

I just wish i could feel nothing especially empathy like a sociopath. That way I might actually be free from this burden of useless feelings i have and finally have transcendence.

teddy

item.title: Alone and Depressed
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I just want to talk to someone,
Or scream how much of a pain i am in.
Even though I try to convince myself that this’ll end
It won’t.
It’s not Physical pain to be treated.
Everytime i see myself in mirror,
I see someone who’s struggling to survive.
I see someone who’s in pain,
I see someone who’s about to end it all.
I wish i had the resolve to go through it,
But i don’t.
I’m in an dilemma,
Should i stay alive and suffer so it might get better
or
Should I just end it all and let myself be free
It’s hard when you have to build from scratch
It’s hard when you have to put up the bread for family
It’s hard to live with delusional parents who thinks that we’re fine
The Truth is we’re not.
They fucked up,
They fucked it up all.
and they are too delusional to admit it.
I just want to leave and start over,
So at least I’ll be able to survive this mess.
I know this doesn’t make sense
But,
I hope you’d understand it.

Note to self - If you ever survive this, thank you for not giving up.

item.title: Writing, Depression and Curses
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
It’s hard to describe a feeling when you’re not feeling it yourself. You can try, but it won’t work. It’ll end up being a version of your most common feeling. You can convince yourself that it is the real feeling but you know that it isn’t. Be it anything good or bad if you can’t feel it for yourself, you can’t.

I write depressing stuff. Mostly because when i got into writing, I was depressed most of the time. So every word I wrote was embedded with it, molded by it and came out as it is. I couldn’t write something Happy even if you held me at a gunpoint. Even now I’m less depressed or used to it now, i still can’t find my way around words. I guess I’m cursed for life and won’t be able to write something Happy after all. But if i write that’s all that matters to me. Writing ease the emotional distress I’m in. It saved me from lots of things and i owe my life to my hobby.

item.title: Dear 17 year old me
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Dear 17 year old me,

I’m sorry but i couldn’t become what you wanted.
I’m not a Singer or a Musician,
You wanted me to Marry by 25,
Well, fuck! It takes a lot to even find someone to marry,
I couldn’t find her, yet.
You were happy, I’m not
You weren’t depressed, I am
You had someone you thought you loved, I don’t.
You were wrong about society of future,
You were wrong about people around you,,
The ones who you thought would stay, they left.
The ones you thought who wouldn’t, they stayed.
You weren’t ready for all the heartbreaks,
You weren’t ready for all the sadness,
You weren’t ready for anything at all,
I don’t blame you,
You tried your best,

But,

Dear 17 year old me, it wasn’t enough.
People used you,
People threw you away,
You changed so you’d be safe,
If you could see what I’ve become,
You’d actually go through that one time.
I’m glad you didn’t,
I’m glad that I’m still here,
The life is still young,
Let’s make the most of it.

Your’s sincerely,
A much older You.

item.title: Spectator
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Someone asked me, what was the biggest sacrifice you’ve made for love. I had to think for awhile. There were many sacrifices i made in the name of love. Although I never managed to love down even one of those sacrifices, at the end of the day there was only one that stood out.

It was to see that my unrequited love being loved by someone else and me being happy for them. I was truly happy for her. She was living the dream. Someone who loves and cares for her, someone who always stood by her side. She never would’ve done half the things she did if i were to intervene. Therefore, the decision i took, the sacrifice i made even though no one really knew, was the biggest i did. And it was pretty much the only sacrifice i can give a worth to.

item.title: Me, but not me.
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I would often wonder how i wrote whatever i wrote back then. Whenever i look at them or read them i don’t see myself writing them at all. It’s like i was possessed by some entity before I wrote what i wrote. Every word and sentence is pure act of someone else but me. It gets a little weird when those writings begin to relate to what i am in today. It’s like i knew where i would be and wanted to take a shot at me when time is ripe. Everytime i read my words, i see someone else other than me.

item.title: Why do you keep coming back to me?
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
“I Loved you.” She insisted.

See, That’s where you’re wrong. you never loved me. you thought you did, but you didn’t. you were looking for validation. at that moment of your life you needed someone weaker or unfortunate than you. you wanted someone to suffer more than you. i was the scapegoat in your quest to find such a victim.

whenever you feel down or insecure you would ask me how my day was, Because you knew my day would’ve been shittier than yours was. it always was.

and then with the time i was moving up through life, i was getting in to good communities, good job, friends that you couldn’t tolerate, i was moving away from your criteria. then your fits, tantrums grew to the point that i had to leave you.

and I did.

So, let me answer my question to you. “Why do you keep coming back to me?”

Because, you still keep seeking that validation from someone. but this time, this time i know better. i am better.

So, sorry that i loved you, even if you didn’t.

item.title: The girl from the web..
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
I wouldn’t exactly call her a ball of pure happiness. she was grey, her heart was pure, but her life filled with darkness. she was someone you would call a flight risk. yet, i found her quite compelling. a puzzle to solve. the way i met her, the way i kept talking with her, i have grown quite fond of her.

She was a paradox, scary yet intriguing.

the day i solve that puzzle, i might able to reach her. hopefully, someday.

item.title: Repetitive Motions
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I wonder why she even bother to come back in to my life again. i was not her ideal guy, i was never. but then again somehow she’s trying to get back to me. i’m not that special. only specialty i ever had was being an ultimate saboteur of relationships. i was a genius at it. and beause of that sole purpose i gave up relationships. not because i suffer or they suffer, because eventually it got boring. people became predictable and life was too clichéd. Somehow i began to see things repeating itself. it’s like my life ran out of things to do. so now it’s trying to bring back blasts from past. so i am reliving things i have experienced before and it’s so boring. Just like how that girl who went away with another guy. And she asked me why did i never asked her out. I was at loss of words. What should I say? I have nothing to say at all. I screwed it up, i screwed it up big time. And i have no excuses.

item.title: Hopes
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Tick tock, Tick tock time’s running out. Every breath i take seems like it’s the last one. I was wondering what I’m gonna do with my life like always. It seems that things won’t go in my way, never. Life was never is, but I wasn’t expecting it to be this hard. If it was just a bit easier so i can sinply manage.

item.title: Writers Block
item.author: Teddy
item.content:
Writers block is the worst thing that can happen to a writer. It keeps happening to me. No matter how much I try to keep up with it all fails. I suddenly lose motivation, my writings get deleted because i forgot to save it or many others reasons that are completely out of ordinary. Still i try because once in a blue moon i write something mind blowing that makes me flip out and ask myself did i do that? So here I am for the nth time and hoping this would stick and hoping I can pull out some rabbits out of hat or at least a carrot. Before I go,

A person who loves you going to keep you to themselves that instead of asking the world from you, world ask you back from them.

item.title: Note to myself..
item.author: Teddy
item.content: I used to be a good writer. But something stopped me from writing. I never wanted to take a pen or open up a blog draft again. I was consumed to a point where i despised reading. I despised every aspect of it. It’s like breaking up with your long-term partner and keep saying you’d do fine without her. Eventually you’ll learn how to do it but you won’t ever be the same again. I wrote this little note as a reminder to myself. So if you’re reading this teddy, don’t give up on writing. That’s where your heart lies.

item.title: Returning to Blogging
item.author: Teddy
item.content: Okay, This Sucks. I’m known to rant about everything but in reality I’m doing it all in my head. No real words, No Audience. I rant to make myself comfortable or to escape from Life itself. I used to be this bright kid who spew sunshine out of my ass or i think i was. I can’t exactly remember as depression sweep me of my feet and made me its bitch. So I decided to rant on a blog which i created 4 years ago, and have 2 posts. that’s how lazy i was. Very.

item.title: Guess What!
item.author: Teddy
item.content: The mere illusion of everlasting love may console you for a while but the life can not built upon that. more you depend from the others you’ll get sucked in to darkness known as empathy. to build your life taste the pain of failures, the hardship of search and the learn to take risks. and most importantly learn the fact that nobody truly cares. This is a battlefield for you to survive. Cheers!
